description: bl-8a9a's in-place migration (state_repo::first_contact + retire_legacy_worktree) captures the legacy balls/tasks via 'git fetch <root> balls/tasks' + 'reset --hard FETCH_HEAD' — only the committed branch tip — then force-removes .balls/worktree (git_worktree_remove with force=true). Any uncommitted state in the legacy worktree is lost without warning. Verified on a throwaway migration: an injected modification to a tracked task JSON and an untracked new task file were both gone afterward. This is a one-time data-loss window on the first post-bl-8a9a run. The live dogfood bare hub is exposed — its .balls/worktree currently carries an uncommitted deletion (bl-04ed.notes.jsonl). Fix: before retiring, detect uncommitted/untracked changes in .balls/worktree and either refuse with a clear message (commit your task state first) or commit them onto balls/tasks so the fetch captures them. Found by the bl-dc2b verification.
title: Migration silently discards uncommitted state in the legacy .balls/worktree
